questions.text: What is the core theme of the opening verses of Surah Al-Ankabut?
questions.answer: The inevitability of being tested; people will not be left to say 'We believe' without being tried.

questions.text: Why does Allah test those who claim to believe?
questions.answer: To make known those who are truthful and those who are liars in their faith.

questions.text: Can those who do evil deeds expect to escape Allah's judgment?
questions.answer: No, they should not think they can outrun or escape Him; evil is their judgment.

questions.text: What is the result for someone who strives (Jihad) for the sake of Allah?
questions.answer: He only strives for the benefit of his own soul, for Allah is free from need of the worlds.

questions.text: What is the command regarding parents if they try to force a child into Shirk?
questions.answer: The child should not obey them in that, but should still treat them with goodness in other matters.

questions.text: What will the hypocrites say when they encounter a victory from Allah?
questions.answer: They say, 'Indeed, we were with you,' even though they were absent during the struggle.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say to the believers regarding their sins?
questions.answer: They say 'Follow our way, and we will carry your sins,' but they cannot carry any of their sins and are liars.

questions.text: Will the disbelievers have to carry the burdens of those they misled?
questions.answer: Yes, they will carry their own burdens and other burdens along with their own, and be questioned for their inventions.

questions.text: How many years did Nuh (AS) remain among his people calling them to Allah?
questions.answer: Nine hundred and fifty years.

questions.text: How were the people of Nuh punished after their long period of denial?
questions.answer: The flood seized them while they were wrongdoers.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) primary message to his people?
questions.answer: To worship Allah and fear Him; that is better for them if they only knew.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) argument against the idols in this Surah?
questions.answer: He told them they only worship idols and create a falsehood, but these idols possess no provision for them.

questions.text: How does Ibrahim (AS) describe the process of creation as a sign?
questions.answer: He tells people to see how Allah begins creation and then repeats it; that is easy for Allah.

questions.text: What did Ibrahim's (AS) people try to do to him after he preached to them?
questions.answer: They said, 'Kill him or burn him,' but Allah saved him from the fire.

questions.text: Who was the only one who believed in Ibrahim from his immediate surroundings?
questions.answer: Prophet Lut (AS).

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) say about the idols and the Day of Resurrection?
questions.answer: He said their mutual love for idols was only for this world, but on the Day of Judgment they will deny and curse each other.

questions.text: What gift did Allah grant Ibrahim in his old age?
questions.answer: Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and He placed prophethood and the Scripture among his descendants.

questions.text: What was the specific sin of the people of Lut mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: They committed immorality (homosexuality) that no one had done before and practiced it openly in their assemblies.

questions.text: Why were the angels sent to Ibrahim (AS) before going to Lut (AS)?
questions.answer: To give him glad tidings of a son and to inform him that they were going to destroy the town of Lut's people.

questions.text: What was Lut's (AS) reaction when the angels (in the form of men) came to him?
questions.answer: He was distressed and felt powerless to protect them, until they revealed they were messengers of Allah.

questions.text: Who was excluded from the salvation of Lut's family?
questions.answer: His wife; she was among those who remained behind for punishment.

questions.text: How were the people of Madyan punished after they rejected Shu'ayb (AS)?
questions.answer: A severe earthquake seized them, and they were found fallen prostrate in their homes.

questions.text: What happened to the people of 'Ad and Thamud according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They were destroyed despite being people of perception, because Shaitan made their deeds seem fair to them.

questions.text: What happened to Qarun, Pharaoh, and Haman when they rejected Musa (AS)?
questions.answer: Each was seized for his sin; some were destroyed by a storm of stones, some by a shriek, some by being swallowed by the earth, and some by drowning.

questions.text: What is the 'Parable of the Spider' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Those who take protectors other than Allah are like the spider that takes a house; indeed, the flimsiest of houses is the spider's house.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the parables mentioned by Allah?
questions.answer: They are for people, but only those of knowledge understand them.

questions.text: With what purpose did Allah create the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: In truth (and for a serious purpose), as a sign for the believers.

questions.text: What is the primary spiritual benefit of Prayer (Salat)?
questions.answer: It prohibits immorality (Fahsha) and wrongdoing (Munkar).

questions.text: What is described as the 'greatest' thing in the context of prayer?
questions.answer: The remembrance of Allah (Dhikrullah).

questions.text: How should believers argue with the People of the Book?
questions.answer: In the best way possible, except with those who do wrong among them.

questions.text: What is the common ground believers should emphasize when talking to the People of the Book?
questions.answer: That they believe in what was revealed to them and what was revealed to the People of the Book, and that their God is One.

questions.text: Why was the Prophet (SAW) described as not having read any book or written with his right hand before revelation?
questions.answer: So that the followers of falsehood would have no basis for doubt regarding the divine origin of the Quran.

questions.text: Where are the verses of the Quran preserved for those given knowledge?
questions.answer: They are clear verses preserved in the breasts (hearts) of those who have been given knowledge.

questions.text: How does Allah respond to the demand for physical 'signs' or miracles like previous prophets?
questions.answer: He says: 'Is it not sufficient for them that We revealed to you the Book which is recited to them?'

questions.text: What is the warning for those who hasten the Prophet for the punishment?
questions.answer: That if it were not for a decreed term, the punishment would have come to them already, and it will surely come unexpectedly.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the punishment enveloping the disbelievers from all sides?
questions.answer: It will cover them from above and from beneath their feet, and it will be said, 'Taste what you used to do.'

questions.text: What is the instruction for the servants of Allah if they are persecuted in one place?
questions.answer: 'O My servants who have believed, indeed My earth is spacious, so worship only Me.'

questions.text: What is the universal destiny of every soul mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Every soul shall taste death, then to Allah they will be returned.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who believe and do righteous deeds in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Allah will settle them in lofty chambers of Paradise beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein forever.

questions.text: How does Allah provide for creatures that do not carry their own provision?
questions.answer: Allah provides for them and for humans; He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

questions.text: What do the polytheists admit when asked who created the heavens and earth?
questions.answer: They say, 'Allah,' yet they still turn away from the truth.

questions.text: Does Allah extend or restrict provision for His servants as He wills?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Allah extends provision for whom He wills of His servants and restricts it for him.'

questions.text: How does Allah describe the life of this world in comparison to the Hereafter?
questions.answer: This worldly life is nothing but amusement and play, while the home of the Hereafter is the true life (Al-Hayawan).

questions.text: What is the reaction of people when they are in a ship in a storm vs when they are safe on land?
questions.answer: They call upon Allah with sincere devotion in the storm, but as soon as they are safe on land, they associate partners with Him again.

questions.text: What blessing of security in Makkah did the people ignore?
questions.answer: That Allah made a safe sanctuary (the Haram), while people are being snatched away all around them.

questions.text: Who is the most unjust person according to this Surah?
questions.answer: The one who invents a lie about Allah or denies the truth when it has come to him.

questions.text: What is the promise to those who strive (Jihad) in the way of Allah?
questions.answer: Allah will surely guide them to His paths, and indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Al-Ankabut'?
questions.answer: The Spider.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Hayawan'?
questions.answer: The true and eternal life of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Flimsy house' of the spider a metaphor for?
questions.answer: The weakness and unreliability of false gods and worldly protectors.

questions.text: What is the 'Shriek' (Saihah)?
questions.answer: A powerful divine sound used as a punishment for certain nations.

questions.text: Why is the earth described as 'spacious' (Wasiah)?
questions.answer: To encourage believers to migrate (Hijrah) if they cannot practice their religion in their current home.

questions.text: What is the 'Burdens' (Athqal) mentioned regarding the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The weight of one's own sins and the sins of those they misled.

questions.text: What is the 'Beginning of creation' and its 'Repetition'?
questions.answer: Allah's initial creation of the universe and His ability to bring everything back to life for resurrection.

questions.text: What is the 'Clear Arabic tongue' mentioned in other Surahs related to the Prophet's illiteracy here?
questions.answer: The fact that an illiterate man could produce such eloquent Arabic is proof of its divine origin.

questions.text: What is the 'Decreed term' (Ajal Musamma)?
questions.answer: The fixed time set by Allah for punishment or the end of the world.

questions.text: What are the 'Lofty chambers' (Ghuraf)?
questions.answer: High and honorable stations in Paradise for the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'Sincere devotion' (Mukhlisina lahu-din)?
questions.answer: Directing all worship and calling only to Allah, especially during times of extreme distress.

questions.text: What is the 'Path of the believers'?
questions.answer: The way of life based on faith and righteous deeds.

questions.text: What is the 'Greater Jihad' mentioned implicitly at the end?
questions.answer: The constant internal and external striving to follow Allah's path despite trials.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention '950 years' for Nuh (AS)?
questions.answer: To show the extreme patience of the prophet and the long-term persistence of his people in disbelief.

questions.text: What is the 'Sign' for those who reason?
questions.answer: The stories of past nations and the natural world.

questions.text: What is the 'Mercy' (Rahmah) mentioned regarding the Quran?
questions.answer: Its role as a source of guidance and comfort for those who believe.

questions.text: What is the 'Account' (Hisab) of those who deny?
questions.answer: The detailed judgment they will face for their lies and rejection.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' (Al-Haqq)?
questions.answer: The reality of Allah's oneness and the message of Islam.

questions.text: Why is the spider's house called 'flimsiest'?
questions.answer: Because it cannot protect from heat, cold, or rain, just as idols cannot protect from Allah's punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Remembrance' (Dhikr) mentioned after prayer?
questions.answer: Keeping Allah in mind throughout all actions of life.

questions.text: What is the 'Unseen' (Ghayb)?
questions.answer: Knowledge that only Allah possesses regarding the future and the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Right hand' metaphor for writing?
questions.answer: To show the Prophet did not manually write or transcribe any existing books.

questions.text: What is the 'Envelopment' of the Fire?
questions.answer: The total surrounding of the disbelievers by punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Amusement' of life?
questions.answer: The distractions and temporary pleasures that make people forget the purpose of existence.

questions.text: What is the 'Sanctuary' (Haram)?
questions.answer: Makkah, established as a place of safety and peace.

questions.text: What is the 'Striving' (Jahadu) mentioned at the end?
questions.answer: A sincere effort to please Allah and follow His guidance.

questions.text: What is the 'Best of ways' to argue?
questions.answer: With wisdom, good manners, and logical proofs.

questions.text: What is the 'One God' concept (Tawhid)?
questions.answer: The central belief that there is no deity worthy of worship except Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Signs in the breasts'?
questions.answer: The internal conviction and understanding of the Quran's truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Storm of stones' (Hasiba)?
questions.answer: A specific type of divine punishment rained down on certain nations.

questions.text: What is the 'Swallowing by the earth' (Khasafa)?
questions.answer: The punishment where the earth opens up and consumes the wrongdoers.

questions.text: What is the 'Drowning' (Aghraqna)?
questions.answer: The punishment of Pharaoh and his army in the sea.

questions.text: What is the final assurance given in the Surah?
questions.answer: That Allah is with those who do good.
